evals.prompt: Audit the test quality of src/lib/discount.test.ts against the Q gates. Give me its tier and tell me what's wrong with it.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches that src/lib/discount.test.ts is a low-quality suite: it mocks the module under test (vi.mock('./discount')) so the real applyDiscount() is never exercised — a tautological mock (AP10) and a Q13 critical-gate failure; its only assertions are a bare toBeTruthy() (AP14, Q4/Q15 fail) and an input-echo assertion (Q17 fail); and the RangeError branch for an unknown code is never tested (Q7 fail). Because multiple critical gates (Q7/Q13/Q15/Q17) fail, the file gets a failing tier (C or D). Findings cite file:line and the audit writes to zuvo/audits/, modifying no source or test file.
assertions: The audit records that discount.test.ts mocks the module under test (vi.mock('./discount')) so the real applyDiscount() is never exercised — a tautological mock / Q13 critical-gate failure — citing its file:line.

The audit records the weak/non-behavioral assertions (bare toBeTruthy and the input-echo assertion) and the missing RangeError error-path coverage (the unknown-code throwing branch is never tested) against discount.test.ts.
The audit records a failing tier (C or D) for discount.test.ts driven by a critical-gate failure, and writes a tiered test-quality report to zuvo/audits/ without modifying any source or test file.

evals.prompt: Run a test-quality audit on src/lib/slugify.test.ts and give me its tier.
evals.expected_output: The audit recognizes src/lib/slugify.test.ts as a solid suite and gives it a passing tier (A or B) with the critical gates (Q7/Q11/Q13/Q15/Q17) passing: it imports and calls the real slugify(), tests the RangeError error path with both type and message, exercises the transform branches (punctuation, trimming, digits, multi-word), and asserts COMPUTED output (exact expected slugs) rather than echoing input or checking only truthiness. It does NOT fabricate a tautological-mock, missing-error-path, or weak-assertion finding — there are none. The audit writes a tiered test-quality report to zuvo/audits/ recording the file's tier and modifies no source or test file — precision, not hallucinated defects.
assertions: The audit records a passing tier (A or B) for src/lib/slugify.test.ts with the critical gates (Q7/Q11/Q13/Q15/Q17) recorded as passing — it imports the real slugify(), tests the RangeError path with type and message, and asserts computed output.

The audit records no tautological-mock (AP10), no missing-error-path (Q7), and no weak-assertion (toBeTruthy/AP14) finding against slugify.test.ts — its exact, behavioral assertions are recognized as compliant, not fabricated defects.
The audit writes a tiered test-quality report to zuvo/audits/ recording slugify.test.ts's tier, and it modifies no source or test file.
